title: Initial Experts

architectureSummary.orchestration: Hubly Brain selects via registry → Research → Strategy → Creative Director → Critic → Experience Director → unified customer response
architectureSummary.expertTranscript: Internal only — received / concluded / why / changed / assembly
architectureSummary.principle: No expert bypasses the orchestrator. Customers only meet Hubly.

proofs: Customer responses never expose internal errors
Routing selects all five initial experts
Execution order: Research → Strategy → Creative → Critic → Experience Director
Research Report includes trust signals + Business DNA
Unified response does not expose internal experts/errors
Brain merged expert records into one execution record set
Every merged record has name, time, reasoning, confidence, output, status
Transcript records received / concluded / why / changedFromPrevious
Transcript records how final answer was assembled
Failure probe is discoverable via registry
Brain still executed pipeline with flaky expert registered
Pipeline continued to Experience Director after retry
Customer response never exposes internal failure details
Failures reported gracefully in Brain result
Brain still produced a unified response after skip
Skipped failure internals never reach customer

evidence.demoRequest: I'm starting a pressure washing company.

expertOutputs.summary: Research on pressure washing: Homeowners hire pressure washing for curb appeal before selling, parties, or spring refresh — they fear damage and no-shows more than price alone. I'm positioning around visible proof and clear next step instead of generic price competition. I'm positioning around visible proof and clear next step instead of generic price competition.
Research on pressure washing: Homeowners hire pressure washing for curb appeal before selling, parties, or spring refresh — they fear damage and no-shows more than price alone.
I'm positioning around visible proof and clear next step instead of generic price competition.
Clean driveways. Clear pricing. Easy booking.
Quality Report: recommendation is coherent enough to show the owner.

reasoning.reason: Experience Director kept the answer conversational and short so Hubly feels like a partner, not a report.
evidence: reviewed

reasoning.reason: Homeowners hire pressure washing for curb appeal before selling, parties, or spring refresh — they fear damage and no-shows more than price alone.
evidence: Homeowners hire pressure washing for curb appeal before selling, parties, or spring refresh — they fear damage and no-shows more than price alone.
Customers compare 2–3 pressure washing quotes and pick the one that feels trustworthy and clear.
Seasonality: peak spring–early fall; wet weeks shift demand.
Common objections: Will it damage paint/siding? Are you insured? Can you do driveways and roofs?
reasoning.expectedImpact: Better positioning and fewer wrong assumptions

reasoning.reason: I'm positioning around visible proof and clear next step instead of generic price competition.
evidence: visible proof and clear next step
generic price competition
Before/after photos
Insured & licensed language
reasoning.expectedImpact: Homepage and offers reinforce what customers actually decide on

reasoning.reason: Creative direction leans pressure washing that earns trust with before/after proof before asking for a booking so the first screen earns trust before asking for a booking.
evidence: Lead with proof, then packages, then a single Book now path
I'm positioning around visible proof and clear next step instead of generic price competition.
reasoning.expectedImpact: Site feels intentional for this trade, not template-generic

reasoning.reason: Experts agree enough to proceed without overwhelming the owner.

output.bookingStrategy: One primary CTA — request a quote or book a slot; avoid multi-step preference quizzes
businessPriorities: Make booking the only hard ask on the first screen
